subject: [COMMON] Run timescale with postgres user
commit_message:
[COMMON] Run timescale with postgres user

Timescale container is ran with postgres user and group which are
defined with uid 70 and gid 70.

Data volume owner is changed for postgres.

See also:
https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

Issue-ID: CPS-667
Signed-off-by: Bruno Sakoto <bruno.sakoto@bell.ca>
Change-Id: Ia87922ba68bb47a7a07aaf61f368143d970278b6

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> I was looking for the gatimg results for path 2 and found this:
> https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/124280-2/
> 
> Does it means that the fix is passing security gating test ?

strange result...
and if you look into them you can see that tests were executed on a lab were ONAP was not installed..so without surprise there is no root pods 😊
infra results show that the lab is not properly installed then security tests are meaningless here. 

as you rebased, you launched another gate (22 in queue when you launch it) so we should have one additional result in few hours
21/09 21h14 
as we have 2 gates => 11 patches to be test before this one => ~ 22h => results for today ~ 19:00
to have faster feedback we need more gating labs as already reported to TSC

It doesn't work. You have still the same issue that I had in the local env:
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124280-5/k8s/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-cps-temporal-db-0-cps-temporal-db-logs.html

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

my bad, I did just have a look at the dashboard
but if we click on the test results => https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124280-5/root_pods/root_pods/root_pods.log

error: Internal error occurred: error executing command in container: failed to exec in container: failed to start exec "9397418d6a066a2448307b472785a2596d86fc95426f5bd8e796e29a180f5c64": OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "id": executable file not found in $PATH: unknown

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> 
> my bad, I did just have a look at the dashboard
> but if we click on the test results => https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//124280-5/root_pods/root_pods/root_pods.log
> 
> error: Internal error occurred: error executing command in container: failed to exec in container: failed to start exec "9397418d6a066a2448307b472785a2596d86fc95426f5bd8e796e29a180f5c64": OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "id": executable file not found in $PATH: unknown

yeah it's there for some time. Our containers got so small that now they sometimes even don't have the id command inside;)

We could fix that by adding other checks like:
cat /proc/self/status | grep Uid

but the problem is that some other containers don't have cat inside xD
So we basically need to either provide our own binary (kubectl cp) or try few methods instead of just one

but in general this is unrelated to this change
